scenes.text: Satellites can't see a wildfire smaller than *a soccer field*
scenes.voiceSegment: Most satellites can't see a wildfire until it's bigger than a soccer field.

scenes.text: This week: 3 FireSat satellites reached orbit
scenes.voiceSegment: This week, three FireSat satellites reached orbit. Built by Muon Space, guided by Google AI, made to catch fires early.

scenes.label: a classroom-sized fire, from 600 km up
scenes.voiceSegment: FireSat spots a fire just five by five meters. A classroom, from six hundred kilometers up, through smoke and clouds.

scenes.imagePrompt: aerial night view of a vast dark pine forest, one single tiny glowing orange ember of fire beginning in the trees far below, thin wisp of smoke, moonlit, dramatic scale contrast between the immense dark forest and the small bright ignition point
scenes.text: The prototype caught a fire other satellites missed
scenes.voiceSegment: The prototype already caught a small Oregon fire other satellites flew right past. Google's AI checks each fire against a thousand past images of the same spot.
The goal: fifty plus satellites, checking all of Earth every twenty minutes. Even hourly checks could save a billion dollars in damage a year, in the U S alone.
The next big fire could be caught while it's small. Follow for daily AI news.
